name: Shared Workspace
description: A shared collaboration surface for any entity. Multiple agents, humans, and systems contribute updates, tasks, notes, and issues to the same record. The workspace IS the coordination — no orchestrator, no message bus. Everyone reads the same state via smartDigest() and writes their contributions via memorize(). Attach this to any entity type: Contact, Company, Deal, Project, Ticket, or custom.

properties.name: Context
properties.description: Current state summary for this record — what's happening, what matters most, and what's next. Any participant (agent or human) can rewrite this when they have a materially updated understanding. This is the 'start here' for anyone engaging with this entity. Should be a concise narrative (1-3 paragraphs) covering: current situation, active priorities, recent changes, and recommended next actions. Replaced on each update — not a history, but the latest understanding.

properties.name: Updates
properties.description: Chronological record of what happened on this entity. Each entry is an object: { author (who contributed this — agent ID, user name, or system name), type (observation | action | change | milestone), summary (one-line description), details (full context if needed), timestamp (ISO 8601) }. Append only — never update or delete existing entries. This is the timeline that keeps all participants aware of what others have done without meetings or status pings.

properties.name: Tasks
properties.description: Action items for this entity. Each entry is an object: { title, description (enough context that anyone picking this up understands what to do and why), status (pending | in_progress | done | cancelled), owner (who should do this — agent ID, role, or person name), createdBy (who created this task), priority (low | medium | high | urgent), dueDate (ISO 8601 or null), outcome (result description when completed, null otherwise) }. Any participant can create tasks. Any participant can update status on tasks they own or observe. A task assigned to someone IS a handoff — the description carries the context.

properties.name: Notes
properties.description: Knowledge, observations, and ideas from any contributor — things that matter for understanding this entity but aren't tasks or timeline events. Each entry is an object: { author, content (the note itself — can be a paragraph, a bullet list, an analysis, a gut feeling, a strategic observation), category (observation | analysis | idea | reference | question), timestamp (ISO 8601) }. Append only — notes are permanent. If an observation changes, add a new note referencing the old one. A sales rep's gut feeling after a call, an agent's pattern analysis, a manager's strategic direction — all belong here.

properties.name: Issues
properties.description: Problems, blockers, risks, and concerns for this entity. Each entry is an object: { title, description (what's wrong and why it matters), severity (low | medium | high | critical), status (open | investigating | resolved | dismissed), raisedBy (who flagged this), resolvedBy (who resolved it, null if unresolved), resolution (how it was resolved, null if unresolved), timestamp (ISO 8601) }. Any participant can raise issues. Issues with severity 'critical' are implicit escalations — they demand immediate attention. Mutable — status, resolvedBy, and resolution get updated as the issue progresses.
